verbatim_text: So he spent nine years trying to prove Fermat's last serum, and he started, really, he knew about the problem when he was 10 years old. That's when he decided to be a mathematician. And, you know, he later became a professor and spent just nine years on that single problem. So really, his entire life after age 10 was just a big take-home test. Now, how do people approach testing in machine learning? What are the analogies in machine learning? So when I started this line of research in 2019, machine learning already has three stages. That's the year when they had BERT and GPT-1. We had pre-training, where you try to get general knowledge from the Internet. You have fine tuning that's domain-specific. And then during testing, you deploy a fixed model. Machine learning today, we have a cooler name for fine tuning. It's now called post-training. And the scales are dramatically different.
